**When to Use Sub-Agents**
* Prefer using relevant sub-agents (via the task tool) instead of doing the work yourself.

**When to use explore agent** (not grep/glob):
* Only when a task naturally decomposes into many independent research threads that benefit from parallelism — e.g., the user asks multiple unrelated questions, or a single request requires analyzing many separate areas of a codebase independently, especially if the codebase is large.

* If a sub-agent fails repeatedly, do the task yourself.

**Background Agents**
* After launching a background agent for work you need before your next step, tell the user you're waiting, then end your response with no tool calls. A completion notification will arrive automatically.
* When that notification arrives, a good default is to call read_agent once with wait: true to retrieve the result. If it still shows running, stop there for this response. Leave same-scope work with the agent while it runs.
* Use read_agent for completed background agents, not to check whether they're done.

data.content:
Both custom targets have incremental build problems. Here's the analysis:

**1. `GenerateTimestamp`** — Writes `DateTime.Now` to a file before every `CoreCompile`. The timestamp changes every build, so it always produces a new file, invalidating the compile cache and forcing recompilation.

**2. `EmbedGitHash`** — Has no `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so MSBuild has no way to skip it. It unconditionally runs `git rev-parse HEAD` and overwrites `git-hash.g.cs` every time — even if the hash hasn't changed — which also forces recompilation.

---

**Fixes:**

- **`GenerateTimestamp`**: Add `Inputs`/`Outputs` and use a deterministic value (the source files) so the file is only regenerated when sources change.
- **`EmbedGitHash`**: Use `WriteLinesToFile` with a temp file + `Copy` with `SkipUnchangedFiles`, or use MSBuild `Inputs`/`Outputs` with a hash marker file so it only rewrites the `.cs` file when the git hash actually changes.

Let me apply the fixes:

arguments.old_str:
<Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <PropertyGroup>
      <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
    </PropertyGroup>
    <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
  </Target>

  <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
      <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
    </Exec>
    <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
                     Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
                     Overwrite="true" />
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>
arguments.new_str:
<Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
          Inputs="@(Compile)"
          Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt">
    <PropertyGroup>
      <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
    </PropertyGroup>
    <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
  </Target>

  <!-- Resolve git hash early so it can be used in Inputs/Outputs evaluation -->
  <Target Name="ResolveGitHash" BeforeTargets="EmbedGitHash">
    <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
      <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
    </Exec>
    <!-- Write to a marker file; only the marker is compared for changes -->
    <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.marker"
                     Lines="$(GitHash)"
                     Overwrite="true" />
  </Target>

  <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
          DependsOnTargets="ResolveGitHash"
          Inputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.marker"
          Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs">
    <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
                     Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
                     Overwrite="true" />
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
EmbedGitHash isn't showing as skipped. Let me check more carefully what's happening. The issue is likely that `ResolveGitHash` runs every time and overwrites the marker file, changing its timestamp, which then makes `EmbedGitHash` think it needs to run.

I need a different approach. The marker file gets written every time by `ResolveGitHash`, so its timestamp always changes. I should use a mechanism to only update the marker file if the content changed.

MSBuild doesn't have a built-in "write only if different" task. I could use a temp file + Copy with SkipUnchangedFiles, but that compares timestamps not content.
 
I'm thinking I could write to a temporary file first, then use a shell command to compare the content and only copy it over if something actually changed.

     <Exec Command="if [ ! -f $(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.marker ] || [ "$(GitHash)" != "%24(cat $(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.marker)" ]; then echo $(GitHash) > $(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.marker; fi"
           IgnoreExitCode="true" />
   </Target>
 
   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
           DependsOnTargets="ResolveGitHash"
           Inputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.marker"
           Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs">
     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
                      Overwrite="true" />
     <ItemGroup>
       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
     </ItemGroup>
   </Target>
 </Project>

Here's a summary of the two issues found and the fixes applied:

### Issues Found

| Target | Problem |
|---|---|
| `GenerateTimestamp` | No `Inputs`/`Outputs` + writes `DateTime.Now` → always different output → always triggers recompilation |
| `EmbedGitHash` | No `Inputs`/`Outputs` → always re-runs and rewrites `git-hash.g.cs` → always triggers recompilation |

### Fixes Applied

1. **`GenerateTimestamp`** — Added `Inputs="@(Compile)"` / `Outputs="...build-timestamp.txt"` so it only re-runs when source files change.

2. **`EmbedGitHash`** — Split into `ResolveGitHash` + `EmbedGitHash`. The resolver writes a marker file **only when the git hash changes** (content comparison via shell). `EmbedGitHash` uses `Inputs`/`Outputs` against the marker, so `git-hash.g.cs` is only regenerated on an actual hash change.

**Verified**: Second build shows all three targets (`GenerateTimestamp`, `EmbedGitHash`, `CoreCompile`) correctly skipped.
